VLM Quick Reference Guide
How to Create a Damage Report
Venue Logistics — FF&E Damage Incident Reporting  |  FIFA World Cup 2026™
The Damage Report (DR) process ensures that any damage occurring at your venue during operations or the event period is properly documented, reported, and traceable. Any item damaged after delivery — whether during offloading, unpacking, assembly, setup, usage, or dismantling — must have a corresponding Damage Report. No damaged item should be moved, discarded, or returned to the warehouse without one.
!
It is important to note that this process does not apply to damages identified during transport or delivery — those must be recorded directly in the Delivery Documents (DD) at the time of receiving.
👤
Who is responsible? The individual who has custody of the item at the time the damage occurs is responsible for initiating the Damage Report. As a VLM, you can create the report directly in SM2ARTA. For venues without a VLM, the report must be completed using the standard Damage Report Form (Excel) and submitted by email to the Material Planning Manager, who will enter it into SM2ARTA on their behalf.
What must be included?
  • The venue name and code
  • The functional area or logical space
  • The affected SKU and quantity
  • The date and time the damage occurred or was discovered
  • A detailed description of the damage and its circumstances
  • The name and role of the person reporting it
📸 Critically, every report must include at least three clear photos of the damaged item — both overall and close-up — along with a written explanation of how the damage occurred and any immediate actions taken.
✓
By following this process consistently, you ensure full traceability and accountability for all damaged assets at your venue.

Step-by-Step Instructions

SM2ARTA Walkthrough
1
Navigate to https://fwc26.smmarta.com/
2
Click "Asset Tracking"
3
Click "Transactions"

Step-by-Step Instructions

Navigation & Form
4
Click "FWC26"
5
Click "Damage report"
6
Select the location of the incident.

Step-by-Step Instructions

Incident Details
7
Select the time/date of the incident.
8
Select the type of incident.
9
Select the damage level of the materials,
© 2026 Vladislav Abramov | SM²ARTA™ Page 4 of 7

Step-by-Step Instructions

Description & Documents
10
Describe the incident, including a written explanation of how the incident occurred. Identify any personnel, equipment, or external factors involved.
11
Type in the corresponding LSR.
12
Click the "Documents" field.
© 2026 Vladislav Abramov | SM²ARTA™ Page 5 of 7

Step-by-Step Instructions

Nomenclatures
13
Click "Add nomenclature" to add the specific items and quantities related to this incident.
14
Click the "Nomenclature name" field and search for the related nomenclatures.
15
Click this text field and enter the quantity of each nomenclature related incident.
16
Click "Add nomenclatures"
© 2026 Vladislav Abramov | SM²ARTA™ Page 6 of 7

Step-by-Step Instructions

Export & Review
17
Return to the related transactions and click the download icon to export the Damage report. Review the export and share it with the relevant parties.
18
Exported Damage Report
